nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Baudry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements bald ‘bold’ + rīc ‘powerful’. Compare Bowdry .
Altered form of French   Beaudry  , a variant of 1 above.

variants.name: Bowdry
meanings: Altered form of French   Baudry  . Compare   Bowdre  .
English: variant of   Baldry  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beaudry
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Andre, Armand, Pierre, Amedee, Gilles, Laurent, Rosaire, Aime, Celine, Charlemagne, Elphege.
French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements bald ‘bold’ + rīc ‘powerful’. Compare Baudry , Beaudrie , and Boudrie .

History: Urbain Beaudry or Baudry dit Lamarche from the Anjou region of France is recorded in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1647. Toussaint Beaudry or Baudry from Velluire in the Poitou region of France is documented in Montreal, QC, in 1670.

variants.name: Beury
meanings: From the vocabulary word (Old English berie). This is one of the less common of the names referring to flowers, fruit, and vegetation that began to be used as given names in the 20th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Baudry von Zulpichgau was born in 0882, his father, Dietrich von Ringelheim, was 29 and his mother, Reginhild Ludmilla von Ringelheim, was 23. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Hawise von Betuwe. He died in 0938, at the age of 56.
